data.title: CMS Chronicle #12: The AI-Native Editor
data.excerpt: 748 commits in 11 days. We shipped a conversational CMS interface, upgraded the richtext editor to TipTap v3, built a media processing pipeline, and discovered a save bug that had been hiding since day one.
data.content:
## The sprint

748 commits. 11 days. It sounds absurd, and it was. But when you're building with Claude Code, the bottleneck shifts from typing to thinking. Every commit is a decision. Every decision is tested. The velocity is real.

## What shipped

### Conversational CMS interface

The chat interface now has 40+ tools. You can create documents, rewrite fields, optimize SEO, manage media, run agents, check links, and deploy — all from a single conversation. It's not a chatbot sitting next to the CMS. It *is* the CMS.

### TipTap v3 richtext editor

We upgraded from TipTap v2 to v3. The migration was non-trivial — `immediatelyRender: false` is now required for SSR, and `shouldRerenderOnTransaction: false` prevents per-keystroke flushSync calls. But the result is a significantly faster, more stable editor.

### Media pipeline

Sharp-based image processing: WebP conversion, responsive variants (400w, 800w, 1200w, 1600w), EXIF metadata extraction, and AI image analysis that generates captions and alt-text in all configured locales.

### The save bug

A bug that had been hiding since the first week of development: the TipTap `onUpdate` handler was firing on mount, marking documents as dirty before the user had changed anything. It took three days to find because the symptoms were subtle — phantom saves, stale content, and occasional data loss.

The fix was a two-line guard: skip the first `onUpdate` call after mount. Simple. Obvious in hindsight. But it required understanding the entire save pipeline from editor state to API to storage adapter.
data.author: Christian Broberg
